ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Indonesian martabak manis (sweet stuffed pancake). FOODON_03315091 (pancake food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pancakes) is the closest parent; no specific code exists for martabak manis or red velvet stuffed pancake variants.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is the full 24cm martabak cut into 10 pieces, weighing approximately 500g, as sold by Smile Martabak on field survey data.

nutrient_source.title: Pancake, plain, prepared from recipe — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18259); adapted with cream cheese filling and Oreo biscuit component estimates from USDA Branded Food entries for Philadelphia Cream Cheese (FDC 454004) and Oreo cookies (FDC 358792)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for red velvet martabak manis with cream cheese and Oreo filling. Values are estimated by weighted component blending: approximately 55% red velvet pancake base (adapted from USDA SR Legacy plain pancake, NDB 18259, adjusted for added cocoa and sugar), 25% Philadelphia cream cheese (USDA Branded Food FDC 454004), and 20% Oreo biscuit pieces (USDA Branded Food FDC 358792). Per-100g values are derived from this blended composition across a 500g total serving weight. This is a medium-confidence estimate; exact restaurant recipe is not publicly disclosed.

gi_source.title: Pancakes, made from mix — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Indonesian martabak manis red velvet with cream cheese and Oreo filling. GI of 67 is adopted from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for pancakes made from mix (GI ~67, medium category; Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The cream cheese filling lowers the effective GI slightly relative to a plain sweet pancake, but the dominant carbohydrate load from the pancake base and Oreo biscuits supports retention of the medium GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (67 × 207.5g carbohydrate in 500g serving) / 100 ≈ 143.5. This is a high-GL food due to the large 500g serving size.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and large serving size
hypertension.guidance: At 210mg sodium per 100g and a 500g serving delivering approximately 1050mg sodium (44% of the 2400mg daily limit), individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one or two pieces and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load and high sugar content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of 67 (medium) and an extremely high glycemic load of approximately 143.5 per full serving, this food causes a substantial postprandial blood glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid consuming the full 500g serving and restrict intake to one or two pieces (50–100g) on an occasional basis only, monitoring blood glucose response carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, and sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of approximately 9.1g saturated fat per 100g (45.5g per full serving), 0.2g trans fat per 100g, and 22g sugar per 100g makes this food a significant contributor to atherogenic lipid profiles; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence, limiting intake to one or two pieces and balancing the meal with fibre-rich vegetables and unsaturated fat sources.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Red Velvet Oreo Stuffed Pancake (Martabak Manis Red Velvet Oreo) is an Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake (martabak manis, also called terang bulan) in a red velvet flavour variant, sold in a 24cm round format cut into 10 pieces and weighing approximately 500g. The pancake base is a thick, fluffy, slightly tangy red velvet batter made with cocoa powder, red food colouring, sugar, eggs, and flour, cooked in a heavy cast-iron mould until the base is golden and the interior is soft and spongy. The filling consists of premium Philadelphia cream cheese spread generously across the hot pancake surface, topped with crushed and chunked Oreo biscuit pieces, and finished with a layer of butter before the pancake is folded and sliced. The result is a rich, indulgent dessert with a contrast of textures: a soft, slightly chewy red velvet pancake exterior, a creamy and tangy cream cheese layer, and crunchy Oreo cookie pieces throughout. The flavour profile is sweet, buttery, and mildly chocolatey with a pronounced cream cheese tang. Nutritionally, this is a calorie-dense, high-carbohydrate, high-fat dessert food. Per 100g it provides approximately 342 kcal, 41.5g carbohydrate (of which 22g is sugar), 17.2g fat (of which 9.1g is saturated), and 5.8g protein. The full 500g serving delivers approximately 1710 kcal, making it a high-energy food unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals managing weight, blood glucose, or cardiovascular risk. The glycemic index is estimated at 67 (medium), but the glycemic load per full serving is extremely high at approximately 143.5, posing a significant postprandial glucose challenge. Clinically, this food is of high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and Cardiovascular Disease, and of moderate concern for those with Hypertension due to its sodium content. It is best consumed as an occasional treat in small portions. This dish is a modern creative variant of the traditional Indonesian martabak manis, popularised in Singapore through Indonesian food stalls and delivery platforms.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for this specific commercial product (Red Velvet Oreo Martabak Manis from Smile Martabak). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component blending of USDA FoodData Central entries for plain pancake (SR Legacy NDB 18259), Philadelphia cream cheese (Branded Food FDC 454004), and Oreo cookies (Branded Food FDC 358792). The GI is adopted from the closest analogous food (pancakes from mix, University of Sydney GI Database). Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible but the exact restaurant recipe proportions are not publicly disclosed.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-blend estimates (55% red velvet pancake base, 25% cream cheese, 20% Oreo pieces) derived from USDA FoodData Central approved sources. Exact recipe proportions from Smile Martabak are not publicly available. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pancake entry and is not reliably estimable from the component blend without laboratory analysis. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy pancake source and the component blend does not yield a reliable estimate. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported across all component sources with sufficient consistency to produce a defensible blended estimate. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 as this food contains no vitamin-C-contributing ingredients; confirmed zero by component analysis — all components (pancake batter, cream cheese, Oreo) contribute negligible or zero vitamin C. 6. The halal status is marked false because Oreo cookies sold in Singapore may contain ingredients not certified halal in all markets, and Philadelphia cream cheese halal certification varies by batch; consumers requiring halal-certified food should verify with the restaurant directly. 7. Glycemic load per serving of 143.5 is calculated as (67 × 207.5g carbohydrate in 500g serving) / 100. This is an exceptionally high GL value reflecting the very large serving size. 8. The food_name has been normalised from the menu-style input '(10pcs) Red Velvet Oreo' to the canonical descriptive English name 'Red Velvet Oreo Stuffed Pancake (10 pieces)' per Veritas naming rules.
